title: delivery_queue.status column is stringly-typed; no enforcement of valid values
description: The delivery_queue table uses a TEXT status column with values 'pending', 'dead'. These strings are scattered across delivery.rs, server.rs, and cli.rs with no shared constant or enum. If a third status (e.g., 'held', 'skipped') is added, all query strings must be updated manually and there is no compile-time check. A pub enum DeliveryStatus { Pending, Dead } with Display/FromStr implementations and sqlx::Type would make the status column type-safe and eliminate the stringly-typed SQL literals. The same applies to relays.status ('pending', 'active', 'rejected').
close_reason: Accepted: ponytail comment in schema SQL.
